what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that declares a typed link between an attachment identifier and a board item in the dragnet-development channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no additional body content
target uses the URI form board://item-7bb519e6
standouts: The target is a board:// URI referencing item-7bb519e6
The attachment_type field classifies the link as board_items
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Includes id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset
References an external board item with a board:// URI scheme
concerns: No payload or descriptive body is present, so the semantic content of the attachment is opaque from this file alone
The relationship direction and cardinality are not stated
notes: A thin, timestamped metadata stub that points a channel attachment at a specific board item.
questions: What content or artifact is actually attached via this record?
Is the attachment bidirectional or managed only from the channel side?
What does attachment_type board_items imply about the expected target shape?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose appears to be recording a board-item attachment, and the file does exactly that as a metadata wrapper.
apparent_themes: Cross-referencing channel memory to board items
intention_signals: It appears intended to persist a reference from a channel memory attachment to a board item so the relationship can be discovered and traversed later.
contribution: It contributes a persistent, typed, timestamped association between a channel attachment and a board item, supporting traceability without embedding the full artifact.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record linking attachment att-5e932fa8 in dragnet-development to board item item-7bb519e6 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to record that a board item is attached to a channel memory entry in the dragnet-development channel.
